text:
In the past few years, deep CNNs have been used widely in computer vision such as image classification, semantic segmentation, object detection and super-resolution. It successfully trained the computer to not only learn features that it finds in the images but also to express it at a deeper level than previous algorithms. All these being possible by training the deep layers of the network and getting the desired outputs in a fully automatic way. Because of the many new advancements in deep CNNs, traditional machine learning has become very minor.

In this paper, we begin by providing some introductory background material regarding the essential building blocks of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) such as input layers, convolution and pooling operations, activation functions, batch normalization, dropout and fully connected layers, as well as the roles played by each one of the components of a CNN. Following this introductory material we proceed with a modern overview of the CNN based literature, examining the developments in the design of CNNs for image classification, object detection, as well as video prediction. In the final part of the paper, we look closer to the stability issues, as well as the methods that have been proposed for the alleviation of these instability issues in deep CNNs, and we suggest possible paths for future investigation regarding this matter.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
